name: namespace_escape
description: Attacker exploits MBEFORE bind flag to inject attacker-controlled directory before trusted /bin in a shared namespace, causing parent process to execute attacker binaries.

external_references.description: Analogical mapping. T1574 parent preferred over T1574.006 (dynamic linker hijacking — wrong mechanism for bind misuse in Plan 9).

x_9front_vector: rfork without RFNAMEG; child calls bind(MBEFORE) on /bin; parent resolves attacker binary first
x_9front_detection: poll /proc/n/ns every 30 seconds; alert on hash change from baseline whitelist
x_9front_mitigation: rfork(RFPROC|RFNAMEG|RFFDG|RFENVG); RFCNAMEG preferred for new services

name: namespace_injection
description: Attacker registers rogue 9P server in /srv before legitimate server starts; privileged process mounts attacker server by name and receives attacker content.

external_references.description: Analogical mapping. T1574 parent: rogue server registration, not dynamic linker hijacking.

x_9front_vector: attacker registers /srv/servicename before legitimate server; victim mounts by name
x_9front_detection: monitor /srv for new entries; diff against baseline; alert on any change
x_9front_mitigation: verify /srv entry ownership before mount; start legitimate servers before accepting connections

name: factotum_rpc_interception
description: Same-uid process writes to /mnt/factotum/rpc during active authentication session, capturing authentication material in transit.

external_references.description: Direct mapping: unsecured credentials intercepted during authentication exchange.

x_9front_vector: same-uid process writes forged messages to /mnt/factotum/rpc; captures authentication ticket
x_9front_detection: monitor /proc/factotum/fd for unexpected readers; detect new processes accessing /mnt/factotum
x_9front_mitigation: dedicated uid per service; exclude /mnt/factotum from namespaces of non-authenticating services

name: factotum_key_theft
description: Same-uid process reads plaintext key file from disk before factotum loads it into memory, obtaining long-term authentication key.

external_references.description: Direct mapping: private keys stolen from disk storage.

x_9front_vector: same-uid process reads $home/lib/factotum or similar plaintext key file at login window
x_9front_detection: eliminated by using auth/secstore — no plaintext keys on disk
x_9front_mitigation: auth/secstore for all persistent key storage; no plaintext key files anywhere on disk

name: 9p_unauthenticated_attach
description: Network client sends Tattach with afid=NOFID (0xFFFFFFFF) to a server that incorrectly accepts unauthenticated attach from non-loopback addresses, gaining full namespace access.

external_references.description: Direct mapping: external remote service accessed without authentication.

x_9front_vector: Tattach afid=NOFID sent to network 9P server; server accepts; root fid established without identity
x_9front_detection: monitor for Tattach with afid=NOFID in server logs; alert on any acceptance from non-loopback
x_9front_mitigation: tlssrv -a flag; reject afid=NOFID from all non-loopback addresses; require authenticated attach for all network 9P

name: 9p_fid_exhaustion
description: Attacker opens fids continuously without Tclunk, filling the server fid table and preventing legitimate sessions from creating new fids.

external_references.description: Direct mapping: endpoint denial of service through resource exhaustion.

x_9front_vector: repeated Twalk creating new fids without Tclunk; fid table fills; server returns error on new fid requests
x_9front_detection: monitor per-session fid count; alert when approaching configurable limit
x_9front_mitigation: per-session fid limit <= 32; session timeout for idle sessions; rate limit new fid creation

name: 9p_replay_attack
description: Attacker captures authenticated Tattach message and replays it to a different server instance or after session expiry, gaining unauthorized access without valid credentials.

external_references.description: Direct mapping: adversary-in-the-middle capturing and replaying authentication material.

x_9front_vector: passive capture of Tattach+auth exchange; replay to same or different server; valid ticket accepted
x_9front_detection: TLS prevents capture; dp9ik session nonces prevent replay — detection is prevention
x_9front_mitigation: TLS required for all network 9P; dp9ik provides session-unique nonces preventing replay

name: proc_memory_read
description: Same-uid process reads /proc/target/mem to extract session keys, factotum keys, or other credentials held in target process memory.

external_references.description: Direct mapping: process injection/inspection to access credentials in memory.

x_9front_vector: open /proc/target/mem; seek to known key offset; read raw bytes; extract credentials
x_9front_detection: no observable trace — prevented by uid isolation only
x_9front_mitigation: dedicated uid per service; no same-uid processes that do not need credential access

name: proc_note_injection
description: Same-uid process writes unexpected note to target process via /proc/target/note, triggering note handler bugs or causing abnormal termination.

external_references.description: Direct mapping: process injection via signal/note delivery mechanism.

x_9front_vector: write unexpected note string to /proc/target/note; trigger note handler with unhandled note name
x_9front_detection: note delivery is transient; partially detected by process crash monitoring
x_9front_mitigation: RFNOTEG at fork gives new note group; dedicated uid prevents cross-uid note injection

name: srv_rogue_registration
description: Attacker registers attacker-controlled 9P server in /srv under a legitimate service name before the legitimate server starts, causing privileged processes to mount attacker server.

external_references.description: Analogical mapping: T1574 parent — hijack execution via rogue server registration, not dynamic linker.

x_9front_vector: create /srv/kfs (or other service name) before legitimate kfs starts; victim mounts attacker server
x_9front_detection: monitor /srv for new or unexpected entries; diff against startup baseline every 60 seconds
x_9front_mitigation: verify /srv entry ownership before mount; alert on any /srv change immediately

name: rfork_namespace_inheritance
description: Child process forked without RFNAMEG shares parent namespace; compromised child calls bind to inject into shared namespace, affecting parent process resolution.

external_references.description: ANALOGICAL mapping. T1611 (Escape to Host) describes container escape to host; 9f-011 describes process escaping namespace isolation via shared namespace inheritance. No Plan 9-specific ATT&CK technique exists.

x_9front_vector: rfork without RFNAMEG; child compromised; child calls bind to modify shared namespace; parent affected
x_9front_detection: poll /proc/n/ns; shared namespace detected when multiple processes have identical ns hash
x_9front_mitigation: rfork(RFNAMEG) always for service children; RFCNAMEG preferred for new services; never RFMEM for services

name: fd_leakage
description: Attacker reads /proc/target/fd to enumerate open file descriptors, discovering open credentials, network connections, or sensitive file handles held by target process.

external_references.description: Direct mapping: file and directory discovery to enumerate open resources.

x_9front_vector: read /proc/target/fd (owner-readable); enumerate open fds; identify credential or sensitive file handles
x_9front_detection: no prevention beyond uid isolation; audit fd table at service startup
x_9front_mitigation: RFCFDG or RFFDG at fork; close unused fds before exec; dedicated uid limits who can read /proc/target/fd
